steps.title: Factor the denominator
steps.content: The denominator $5x + 45$ has a greatest common factor of 5: $$5x + 45 = 5(x + 9)$$

steps.title: Simplify and find b
steps.content: Substitute the factored form and simplify: $$\frac{30}{5(x+9)} = \frac{6}{x+9}$$ by dividing numerator and denominator by 5. Comparing to $\frac{6}{x+b}$, we have $x + 9 = x + b$, so $b = 9$. That confirms A is correct.
